steps.title: Identify the calendar benefit
steps.content: The student wants to explain an advantage of the Hanke-Henry calendar. The notes say it has 364 days, which can be divided evenly by 7, so dates fall on the same weekday every year.

steps.title: Reject incomplete background
steps.content: Choice A only compares the number of days in the Gregorian and Hanke-Henry calendars, without explaining why the difference helps. Choice B says the calendar would solve a problem, but it is too vague to explain the advantage. Choice D gives background about the Gregorian calendar’s current use rather than the Hanke-Henry benefit.

steps.title: Link design to scheduling
steps.content: The sentence that connects fixed weekdays to more predictable scheduling uses the relevant information from the notes. It explains why the Hanke-Henry design is advantageous compared with the Gregorian calendar. The answer is C.
